channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: DepEd Expands School Safety Measures with Nationwide Active Threat Drills
item.description: The Department of Education (DepEd) is conducting nationwide active threat drills as part of the National Safe Schools Summit, aimed at enhancing preparedness and ensuring student safety in schools. T...

item.title: Kanga Cup: Kids and Coaches Navigate Wet Pitches and Celebrations
item.description: The Kanga Cup, Australia's largest youth football tournament in the southern hemisphere, faced challenges with rain-soaked pitches and freezing temperatures, but teams and parents embraced the chaos w...

item.title: Sarmiento Appointed to GPH Peace Panel for MILF Negotiations
item.description: Mel Senen Sarmiento was appointed as the concurrent chairperson of the Government of the Philippines (GPH) peace panel for the Mindanao Islamic Liberation Front (MILF) peace process, following the res...

item.title: Data Breach at India's Largest Nuclear Plant Reveals Sensitive Information
item.description: A significant data breach has occurred at India's Kudankulam Nuclear Power Plant, with sensitive files including blueprints, supplier details, and insurance policies leaked online by the ransomware gr...

item.title: Trump's Pressure on Canada Over Bridge Toll Sparks Diplomatic Concerns
item.description: The article discusses Donald Trump's aggressive approach to international relations, exemplified by his demands on Canada regarding the Gordie Howe Bridge. Canada, which funded the $6.4-billion bridge...

item.title: BPI App Experiencing Technical Issues
item.description: The BPI app encountered technical difficulties on July 15, 2026, affecting user access to banking services. The incident was reported by Benise Balaoing of ABS-CBN News, highlighting disruptions in di...

item.title: New Regulations Impact Buy Now Pay Later Services
item.description: The UK is implementing stricter regulations for Buy Now Pay Later (BNPL) services, requiring lenders to obtain Financial Conduct Authority (FCA) approval. These changes aim to protect consumers by enh...

item.title: Fifi Cooper Suspends National Tour Amid Gender-Based Violence Concerns
item.description: South African rapper Fifi Cooper has temporarily suspended her '1 Night With Fifi Cooper Tour' following a personal matter related to gender-based violence (GBV). The decision, announced by her record...

item.title: Sonam Wangchuk's Protest and Government Response
item.description: The article discusses the ongoing protest led by Sonam Wangchuk, a prominent figure in Jammu and Kashmir, who is demanding the restoration of the region's special status. The protest has drawn attenti...

item.title: Nigeria to Lead Humanitarian Efforts with UN Support, Minister Announces
item.description: Nigeria plans to take a leading role in coordinating humanitarian responses within the country, shifting from a system primarily led by international donors and UN agencies. This transition was outlin...

item.title: Iran and US Escalate Tensions Over Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) has reportedly taken unprecedented measures to secure the strategic Strait of Hormuz, a critical waterway for global oil trade. This move comes amid height...

item.title: Kamaru Usman Faces Dricus du Plessis in UFC Fight Night 238 Main Event
item.description: Kamaru Usman, a former UFC welterweight champion known for his dominant 19-0 record, is set to face Dricus du Plessis at UFC Fight Night 281 on July 18 in Oklahoma City. This high-stakes matchup carri...

item.title: South Africa Enhances Preparedness for Potential Ebola Outbreak
item.description: The South African Department of Health has intensified its readiness measures for a possible Ebola outbreak, based on scientific risk assessments. Although no confirmed cases exist in the country, the...

item.title: INEC closes portal as three parties delay candidate submissions
item.description: Three political parties—APGA, PDP (Wike faction), and LP (Nenadi faction)—haven't uploaded their presidential and vice-presidential candidates to INEC's portal before the deadline. INEC extended the s...

item.title: Nigerian Engineer Builds Functional G-Wagon Replica for Sisters' School Transport
item.description: A young Nigerian man, known as Engineer Godday on TikTok, has created a fully functional Mercedes-Benz G-Wagon replica from scratch. The homemade vehicle, which runs on fuel, serves as a daily school ...

item.title: Two Men Arrested for Possession of Explosives in Free State
item.description: Two men have been arrested in Meloding, Free State, after police discovered 11 cone-pack explosives at a residence during an intelligence-led operation. The suspects, aged 37 and 47, were detained fol...

item.title: Pakistan strengthens SCO ties through economic and regional collaboration
item.description: Deputy Prime Minister Ishaq Dar held a productive meeting with Shanghai Cooperation Organisation (SCO) Secretary General Nurlan Yermekbayev, focusing on enhancing cooperation among member states in ke...

item.title: Airtel Africa Boosts Share Buyback Amid Stock Surge
item.description: Airtel Africa Plc has executed a significant share buyback, acquiring 1,855,779 ordinary shares between July 6 and July 10, 2026, under its repurchase programme announced on May 22, 2026. This brings ...

item.title: Senior police officer injured after alleged robbery at Sydney bottle shop involving teenagers
item.description: A senior police officer was taken to hospital after allegedly being assaulted during an incident involving a group of teenage suspects at a bottle shop in Sydney. According to the available informatio...

item.title: Tamil Nadu CM Vijay's Anti-Corruption Stance and Response to Karur Stampede
item.description: Tamil Nadu Chief Minister C Joseph Vijay addressed a rally in Karur, emphasizing strict measures against corruption. He urged citizens to refuse bribes and vowed to eliminate corrupt practices in the ...
